title: Mellow Spicy Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A peppery, fluffy, and cozy Moroccan dish featuring vin-santo, cooked in a Dutch oven, with ayurvedic sattvic friendly ingredients. This recipe is perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the vin-santo, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the oven.
Cook the tagine for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Traditionally, the tagine is cooked in a clay pot with a conical lid, but a Dutch oven is a good substitute.
